ops.text: Fix ClassCastException, survive Android 14 permissions, and optimize Forge script

ops.find_block:

ops.replace_with:
return try {
            val internalDex = File(context.codeCacheDir, "payload.dex")
            dexFile.copyTo(internalDex, overwrite = true)
            // Android 14+ Security: DEX files must be read-only
            internalDex.setReadOnly()
            
            val classLoader = DexClassLoader(
                internalDex.absolutePath,

ops.find_block:
echo "⚔️ FORGING (Kotlin -> Bytecode)..."
# Note: We include the Shell's DynamicEntry.kt in the compilation so the payload knows the interface
$WORKDIR/kotlinc/bin/kotlinc \
    dynamic_src/ \
    app/src/main/java/com/speedster/DynamicEntry.kt \
    -cp "$CP" \
    -Xplugin=$WORKDIR/compose-compiler.jar \
    -d $CLASSES_DIR
ops.replace_with:
echo "⚔️ FORGING (Kotlin -> Bytecode)..."
# Fix: Don't include DynamicEntry.kt in the source list, it must only be on the classpath
# We assume the shell app was built once so we can steal its classes
SHELL_CLASSES="app/build/tmp/kotlin-classes/release"

$WORKDIR/kotlinc/bin/kotlinc \
    dynamic_src/ \
    -cp "$CP:$SHELL_CLASSES" \
    -Xplugin=$WORKDIR/compose-compiler.jar \
    -d $CLASSES_DIR

ops.text: Inject Dynamic Resource Support via AssetManager reflection

ops.find_block:
echo "🔩 CRUNCHING (Bytecode -> Dex)..."
BUILD_TOOLS_DIR=$(ls -d ${ANDROID_SDK_ROOT}/build-tools/34.* | head -1)

$BUILD_TOOLS_DIR/d8 \
    --output $DEX_DIR/ \
    --lib ${ANDROID_SDK_ROOT}/platforms/android-34/android.jar \
    $(find $CLASSES_DIR -name "*.class")

echo "✅ PAYLOAD READY: $DEX_DIR/classes.dex"
ops.replace_with:
echo "🔩 CRUNCHING (Bytecode -> Dex)..."
BUILD_TOOLS_DIR=$(ls -d ${ANDROID_SDK_ROOT}/build-tools/34.* | head -1)

$BUILD_TOOLS_DIR/d8 \
    --output $DEX_DIR/ \
    --lib ${ANDROID_SDK_ROOT}/platforms/android-34/android.jar \
    $(find $CLASSES_DIR -name "*.class")

echo "🎨 PACKAGING RESOURCES..."
# We use aapt2 to link the compiled DEX and any resources into a dummy APK
# This allows Android's AssetManager to 'see' drawables inside the file.
$BUILD_TOOLS_DIR/aapt2 link \
    -I ${ANDROID_SDK_ROOT}/platforms/android-34/android.jar \
    --manifest app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml \
    -o $DEX_DIR/payload.apk \
    $DEX_DIR/classes.dex

# Move it back to classes.dex so your Supabase script doesn't break, 
# but now it's actually a zip-aligned container with a resources header.
mv $DEX_DIR/payload.apk $DEX_DIR/classes.dex

echo "✅ HYBRID PAYLOAD READY (Code + Resource Container)"
